subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Taylor / Maclaurin Series
stimulus:
The function f has a Taylor series about x (cid:32) 1 that converges to f(cid:11)x(cid:12) for all x in the interval of convergence.
It is known that f(cid:11)1(cid:12) (cid:32) 1, f(cid:132)(cid:11)1(cid:12) (cid:32) (cid:16) 1 , and the nth derivative of f at x (cid:32) 1 is given by f (cid:11)n(cid:12)(cid:11)1(cid:12) (cid:32) (cid:11)(cid:16)1(cid:12)n (cid:11)n (cid:16)1(cid:12)!
2 2n
for n (cid:149) 2.

parts.prompt: Write the first four nonzero terms and the general term of the Taylor series for f about x (cid:32) 1.

parts.prompt:
The Taylor series for f about x (cid:32) 1 has a radius of convergence of 2. Find the interval of convergence.
Show the work that leads to your answer.

parts.prompt:
The Taylor series for f about x (cid:32) 1 can be used to represent f(cid:11)1.2(cid:12) as an alternating series. Use the first
three nonzero terms of the alternating series to approximate f(cid:11)1.2(cid:12).

Show that the approximation found in part (c) is within 0.001 of the exact value of f(cid:11)1.2(cid:12).
